title: Packing iCE40 $alu+$mux cells into one LUT4
body:
Currently, `ice40/arith_map.v` is not using `SB_LUT4` primitives very efficiently. That is, `I0` is not used at all, and is tied to `0`:
```verilog
		SB_LUT4 #( ... ) adder (
			.I0(1'b0),
			.I1(AA[i]),
			.I2(BB[i]),
			.I3(C[i]),
			.O(Y[i])
		);
```
However, that LUT row could be used to perform one of the following functions...
  * output constant 0,
  * output constant 1,
  * output A,
  * output B,
... effectively packing a pair of `$alu`+`$mux` cells into a single `SB_LUT4`. This is quite important for small CPUs such as the one described in the [PicoBlaze](http://bleyer.org/pacoblaze/picoblaze.pdf paper).

I was able to design a [prototype](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/2645463/alu.zip) using the `extract` and `techmap` commands that seems to do the trick. However, there are multiple issues with it.
  * The `extract` command does not appear to provide any way to _infer_ parameters from the design. I had to fix `A_WIDTH` etc to 8, which is obviously suboptimal.
  * The `extract` command appears to require extremely verbose definitions. For example, I would need four different cells (of which the sample design currently uses two), to handle the four cases where:
    * mux input low selects ALU Y, mux input high selects ALU A
    * mux input low selects ALU Y, mux input high selects ALU B
    * mux input low selects ALU A, mux input high selects ALU Y
    * mux input low selects ALU B, mux input high selects ALU Y

    and (I think?) it is only possible to unify them into one cell in `techmap` by going through an additional layer of techmap indirection.
  * The `extract` command appears to not recognize subtraction unless `-constports` is provided, which is I understand slow.

Do I understand it correctly that the proper way to work around these is to add an extraction pass in C++?

I would like to test such a pass (no matter how implemented) using formal equivalence checking. Could you point me to any way to integrate such a test into the Yosys testsuite? I'd want to make sure that both (a) the `alu_mux` cells are extracted, and (b) the result is equivalent to the original design. The included `synth_equiv.ys` script tests (b), but I do not understand how to test (a).

Aside: I found that if I ran `techmap -map +/ice40/cells_sim.v`, Yosys would hang (or otherwise work for a very long time, but if I extracted the definitions for `SB_LUT4` and `SB_CARRY` from `cells_sim.v` and ran `techmap -map cells_sim_reduced.v`, this would finish quickly. Is this a bug?
